location: West Lake, Hangzhou, Zhejiang Province, China
historical_period: Original built in 975 AD during the Five Dynasties period
cultural_significance: Leifeng Pagoda is one of the most iconic landmarks of Hangzhou, standing on the southern shore of West Lake. It is famous for its association with the legend of the White Snake and offers panoramic views of West Lake.

architecture.materials: Brick and stone with wooden eaves
architecture.design: Traditional Chinese pagoda architecture with upturned eaves

highlights: Panoramic view of West Lake from the top
Exhibition of relics from the original pagoda

legend.title: Legend of Leifeng Pagoda
legend.description: According to legend, the White Snake Spirit (Bai Suzhen) was trapped under Leifeng Pagoda by the monk Fahai. Her son, Xu Xianxia, eventually rescued her, and the pagoda collapsed. This tragic love story is one of the Four Great Classical Novels in Chinese literature.

history.event: Original pagoda built by King Qian Chu of Wuyue Kingdom
New Leifeng Pagoda rebuilt with elevator and exhibition halls

visiting_info.best_time: Sunset for beautiful views of West Lake
